name: Vercel preview URL
description: Get a pull request's Vercel preview deployment URL with zero Vercel access — the agent polls the vercel[bot] comment on the PR with its own GitHub App token and extracts the link.
setup:
No Vercel credentials are granted or needed — the preview URL is read from the comment Vercel's GitHub integration posts on every pull request. Two things must be true on the provider side:

1. The repository is connected to a Vercel project (the Vercel GitHub App installed on the org with access to the repo, and the project's Git integration pointing at it). PR comments are on by default; leave them enabled in the Vercel project's Git settings.
2. Vercel only builds previews for commit authors it recognizes. Commits authored as the agent's own GitHub App bot identity (`<slug>[bot]`) count as properly-identified bot commits and deploy without a Vercel team seat — commits from an unrecognized user account are blocked with a "must be a member of the team" comment until a Vercel team member authorizes them.

Pairs with the GitHub bundle: the skill reads PR comments using `gh` and the agent's GitHub App credentials (the GitHub App auth skill covers the token mechanics and the commit-attribution rules that satisfy point 2).
